stamp.subLine: The Design District. Where the walk begins.

story.headline: Dramatic from the moment you sit down.
story.body: Forever Fiore is floral, unhurried, a little theatrical. The tea menu alone takes five minutes to read. Order something you have never heard of. The preserved florals around you last three to five years — the owner designed the space to feel timeless, and it does. Good place to start a long morning.
story.insiderTip: Closed Monday and Saturday. Sunday brunch here before the walk is the move.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on 10th Street N in the Design District.

hours.note: Afternoon only — not an all-day café

stamp.subLine: Hidden in the alley. The scones are made this morning.

story.headline: You have to know it's there.
story.body: Brambles is off 5th Avenue with no visible signage from the street. Inside: 75 teas, scratch scones with clotted cream, shrimp Davina on a fresh-baked croissant. It has been here longer than most of the boutiques surrounding it. The kind of place that rewards the people who find it.
story.insiderTip: Order the Brambles Delight. Tea sandwiches, scones with clotted cream and strawberry preserves, delicate pastries, pot of your choice. That is the whole point.
story.parkingNote: 5th Avenue South public parking garage on 8th St S.

hours.note: Open early — best before the boutiques open

stamp.subLine: The corner that started it all.

story.headline: The anchor of 5th Avenue South.
story.body: Corner location, full diner menu, coffee from early morning. 5th Avenue Coffee Company has been the gathering point of this block longer than most businesses here. The milkshakes are not a joke. Neither is the breakfast. Go early before the shopping crowd arrives and the patio fills up.
story.insiderTip: The corner patio is peak people-watching before 10 AM. After that it gets busy.
story.parkingNote: 5th Avenue South public parking garage on 8th St S.

hours.note: Open latest on 5th — coffee to wine without moving

stamp.subLine: Coffee now. Wine later. Same table.

story.headline: The one that stays open.
story.body: Brick walls, living room furniture, a patio facing the full sweep of 5th Avenue. The Brick serves Lavazza coffee in the morning and pivots to beer and wine by afternoon. It is the stop where you sit down for a cortado and look up to find an hour gone. Prime location for 5th Avenue Art Walk nights if the timing works out.
story.insiderTip: Check the 5th Avenue Art Walk calendar before you go. The patio on event nights is worth the detour.
story.parkingNote: 5th Avenue South public parking garage on 8th St S.

stamp.subLine: Italian in 900 square feet. Everything is picture perfect.

story.headline: The newcomer that already belongs.
story.body: Bonta opened in 2024 and came ready. Traditional Italian bakery, 900 square feet, overlooking 5th Avenue. The gelato is serious. The paninis are serious. The pastries are the kind you order two of, because you will wish you had. It fits the block like it has always been here.
story.insiderTip: Get the gelato. Do not skip it because you think you are here for coffee. Get the gelato.
story.parkingNote: 5th Avenue South public parking garage on 8th St S.

stamp.welcomeLine: You made it to The Cafe.
stamp.subLine: Halfway home. The pier is closer than it feels.

story.headline: Bright food, bright morning.
story.body: The Cafe has Scandinavian roots and shows it. Good ingredients, simple preparation, no fuss. Sit on the corner patio at 8th and Fifth and watch Naples wake up. Healthy bowls, smoked salmon plates, pastries that locals have been coming back for since 2013. This is your turn-the-corner stop. Last coffee on 5th before you head to 3rd Street South.
story.insiderTip: Order the Blue Boost. Matcha, butterfly pea, vanilla, lavender, oat milk. The drink locals keep off Instagram.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on 5th; free public garage two blocks east on 6th St S.

hours.note: Open since 1995 — the OG of 3rd Street South

stamp.subLine: Here since 1995. The courtyard is waiting.

story.headline: Old Naples doesn't get more Old Naples than this.
story.body: Tony's has been here since 1995. Wine bar, artisan bakery, coffee bar, gourmet market, all around a courtyard with a fountain. The pastries are made from scratch every morning. The coffee is always right. Thirty years on 3rd Street and the place still feels like it is exactly where it belongs.
story.insiderTip: The croissants are gone by mid-morning. Go early or go without.
story.parkingNote: 3rd Street South has metered street parking. Cambier Park lot nearby.

hours.note: Opened October 2025 — Naples' first indie bookstore in years

stamp.subLine: 16,000 books and a children's cove. You'll stay.

story.headline: Naples needed this. Naples got it.
story.body: Books on Third opened in October 2025 as the first independent bookstore Naples had seen in years. 2,961 square feet, 16,000 books, reading nooks, a children's cove, gifts that pair with what you just bought. It sits upstairs from Ridgway Bar and Grill, which means the building has been a cultural anchor for decades. The bookstore made it complete.
story.insiderTip: The children's cove is genuinely great. Traveling with kids, this is the stop that earns you the most goodwill.
story.parkingNote: 3rd Street South metered parking. Cambier Park lot is closest.

hours.note: 90% open-air seating — best in good weather

stamp.subLine: Open air. Organic. Reader's Choice every year.

story.headline: Garden vibes, award-winning brunch, zero pretense.
story.body: Jane's has won Reader's Choice Best Brunch for nearly a decade. The menu is organic and British-American, the seating is 90% open-air, and the place makes you feel welcome without trying to. A coffee here in the shade of a Naples morning is the right pace for this part of the walk. The organic menu changes seasonally.
story.insiderTip: Ask what is new on the menu. They are usually excited to tell you.
story.parkingNote: 3rd Street South metered parking. Cambier Park lot nearby.

hours.note: Breakfast diner hours — early bird gets the bay view

stamp.subLine: Ten stops. Two streets. The pier is coming back. Walk to the water — the sunset is still there.

story.headline: The last stop before the water.
story.body: The Cove Inn Coffee Shoppe is exactly what it looks like — a cash-only diner inside a small bayfront hotel, run by people who've been doing it the same way for decades. Order the Barge breakfast burrito. Sit by the window. Look at the bay. When you're done, walk west on Broad Avenue to 12th Avenue South. The Naples Pier has been there since 1888 and is being rebuilt right now — sixth time after Hurricane Ian. The construction fence is worth seeing. The beach at 13th Avenue South is open and the sunset is the same one it's always been.
story.insiderTip: Cash or check only — ATM on site. Get here before 9 AM on weekends or you'll wait. The Barge breakfast burrito is the order.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Broad Ave S. Naples Bay is right across the street.

hours.note: Saturday only, 7:30–11:30 AM year-round. One exception per year for Naples St. Patrick's Day parade.

story.headline: Saturday morning, the way it's supposed to be.
story.body: Since 1994, the Third Street South Farmers Market has taken over the parking lot behind Tommy Bahama every Saturday morning. Nearly 60 vendors — fresh produce, Colombian arepas, mushrooms, pastries, fresh-roasted coffee, cut flowers, and more. The market draws locals who have been coming for thirty years and visitors who stumble in and never leave. Live music, dogs everywhere, the full Naples Saturday energy. Opens at 7:30 — get there early.
story.insiderTip: The Argentine empanadas from Casa de Loro are the move. If you miss them, you'll think about it.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on 13th Ave S and Broad Ave. Gets tight after 9 AM — walk from 3rd Street if you can.
